qas.q: What does MMPA stand for?
What does DMO stand for?
What does CMS stand for?
What does DJMS stand for?
What is DJMS based on?
qas.a: The concept of a centralized pay system.

qas.q: Where are all Air Force pay accounts maintained at?
qas.a: DFAS, with each base providing service to their personnel.

qas.q: What is used to maintain military pay records?
What are the directives used by the Air Force for DMJS guidance?
Within AFMAN 65-116, Vol. 1. DJMS-AC FSO Procedures, what lists the commands available on-line for DJMS?
What are the most common inquiries used in DJMS on a daily basis?
What is the command 'JJAA' used for?
qas.a: On-line MMPA inquiries on individual members.

qas.q: A member's inquiry that is accessed by the 'JJAA' command would contain what?
qas.a: Detailed data from a member's current-year pay records plus all previous years back to 1977.

qas.q: How could a technician determine a member's current leave balance?
qas.a: By entering the appropriate codes on the JUMPS MMPA Inquiries screen.

qas.q: What doe JWMM allow?
qas.a: On-line viewing of DJMS tables that show entitlement amounts, federal tax charts, and other pertinent information.

qas.q: What does the Air Force provide to all active duty members at teh end of each month?
qas.a: An itemized statement of leave and earnings.

qas.q: What does the central-site computer maintain and where is it located?
qas.a: A master file on each Air Force member's pay account. At DFAS.

qas.q: How would you access the master file for a specific Air Force member's pay account?
qas.a: By using DJMS and the command JJAA.

qas.q: What is one of the most important tools that the military pay technician has to verify and research a member's pay?
The MMPA contains administrative data, entitlement data, deductions information and payment history. What are some examples of those?
qas.a:
Entitlements, Payments,
Deductions, Statuses,
Allotments, Leaves,
Collections, History of the past 12 months.

qas.q: What does the inquiry print contain?
qas.a: Header information followed by the body of the print with Format Identification (FID) entries including open and/or closed entries.

qas.q: When inquiring an MMPA, what is important to remember?
Chan changes be made to an MMPA from this system?
The first three lines of every inquiry print are referred to as what?
Within a header, which line would the privacy act statement and a current MMPA as of date (YY/MM/DD) identifying how current the information is on the print be on?
Within a header, which line would the member's Social Security Number, the name identification, the paygrade, and the type of request be on?
When viewing a header, how many letters make up the name identification and what are they?
qas.a: The first five letters of the last name.

qas.q: Within a header, which line would the current processing month for DFAS and the FIDS be on?
MJ stands for what?
MJ is normally blank unless what?
qas.a: The member recently came on active duty.

qas.q: LB stands for what?
qas.a: Member's paying accounting and disbursing station number (ADSN).

qas.q: If a member has their paycheck going to a financial institution, the ADSN will always be what?
qas.a: 3800 which is the ADSN for DFAS.

qas.q: If a member is a local payee (not on direct deposit), the paying ADSN is what?
qas.a: The base where the member is currently assigned.

qas.q: LC stands for what?
What is the member's servicing ADSN?
qas.a: The ADSN of the base who maintains the MMPA.

qas.q: SA stands for what?
What does the current status indicator tell you?
qas.a: If the member is on-station (A), Absent Without Leave (AWOL) (J), Leave (B), etc.

qas.q: SX stands for what?
Can a member be in more than one status?
TK stands for what?
What is the Military Pay Date?
TU stands for what?
What is the Date of Separation?
qas.a: The date the member separates from the military.

qas.q: TH stands for what?
What is the Expiration Term of Service?
qas.a: The date the member's current enlistment ends. Normally the same as DOS.

qas.q: The MMPA is organized by categories called what?
FIDs are made up of how many characters and what may they be?
qas.a: Two characters. Alpha, Numeric, or both.

qas.q: In some cases, the first character of a FID identifies what?
The second character of a FID would identify what?
Where could you find all FIDs and categories?
Entries for FIDs will be interpreted in one of two ways. What are those?
Open entries are what?
qas.a: Entries currently in effect on the MMPA.

qas.q: What do Open Entries usually contain?
qas.a: A start or effective date codes to identify information and entitlement or deduction ammounts.

qas.q: What will Entry Open Data (EOD) field identify?
What is a Closed Entry?
qas.a: Entries that are no longer in effect on the MMPA.

qas.q: Close Entries will have an Entry Closed Data (ECD) field to indicate what?
What is the one major difference between closed and open entries?
qas.a: A closed entry will have a hyphen following the FID indicating that an entry was there at one time but no longer has an effect on a member's pay.

qas.q: The Entry Open and Entry Closed Data consists of how many positions identifying when an MMPA entry was originally created or closed?
All current month closed entries will contain what?
Positions 1-6 are what?
Positions 7 and 8 are what?
Positions 9 and 10 are what?
Position 11 is what?
qas.a: Flag (prior to mid-month flag 1 and after mid-month flag 2).

qas.q: What does IAS stand for?
True or False. All inquiries are shown within immediate access storage.
DJMS will provide information for what timeframe?
qas.a: The current month as well as the prior 11 months.

qas.q: If you need to make inquiries outside of IAS, you may request history MMPAs by changing what?
qas.a: The entry on the DJMS inquiry screen.

qas.q: How may types of request are there?
What are the types of requests?
qas.a: FID Request, Group/Category Request, and Table Request.

qas.q: A FID Request contains how many characters?
A FID Request gives you what?
qas.a: Information pertaining to one particular FID.

qas.q: Which request will normally show you the least amount of information? Why?
qas.a: A FID Request. Because you are only looking at one particular FID.

qas.q: A Group/Category Request contains how many characters?
What will a Group/Category Request show?
qas.a: A group of related information, such as entitlements, deductions, taxes, or allotments.

qas.q: Where are categories listed?
A Table Request contains how many characters?
What are the steps for a Table Request?
qas.a: You simply enter a "T" followed by the two-position table number.

qas.q: When are Table Request inquiries used?
qas.a: When you want to examine a member's MPAA due to a particular situation such as separation, retirement, new arrival on base, or a non-pay status situation.

qas.q: Who pre-established (created) table requests?
